description: Production-grade color palettes curated for the retro-futuristic world of Vespera. Inspired by the chromatic systems of Moebius, Syd Mead, and space-age Googie architecture.

palettes.name: Moebius Twilight & Desert
palettes.origin: Inspired by the surreal, pastel-saturated landscapes of Moebius (Jean Giraud) in works like Arzach and The Airtight Garage.
palettes.aesthetic_notes: Characterized by soft, dreamlike sand and sagebrush tones contrasted against cold, highly saturated lavender and electric turquoise highlights. Captures the 'Ligne Claire' comic aesthetic.

colors.name: Sand Drift
colors.usage: Use for primary background structures, desert expanses, and organic rock formations.

colors.name: Sagebrush
colors.usage: Perfect for secondary machinery panels, foliage, and structural middle-grounds.

colors.name: Twilight Lavender
colors.usage: Use for cast shadows, evening skies, and shaded rock alcoves.

colors.name: Electric Turquoise
colors.usage: Reserved for Vespera's hair highlights, neon terminal panels, and active thruster rings.

colors.name: Terracotta Clay
colors.usage: Use for apparel straps, leather flight gear accents, and sunset atmospheric haze.

colors.name: Midnight Indigo
colors.usage: Use for fine ink outlines, dark mechanical framing, and deep spatial voids.

palettes.name: Syd Mead Aerodynamic
palettes.origin: Inspired by the sleek, high-luster industrial designs, architectural concepts, and chrome reflections of Syd Mead.
palettes.aesthetic_notes: A high-end, functional palette combining cool slate-steel bases with polished chrome highlights and hot sunset-amber and cyan interfaces.

colors.name: Slate Steel
colors.usage: Use for primary metal bulkheads, aerodynamic vehicle hulls, and reinforced columns.

colors.name: Polished Chrome
colors.usage: Use for specular highlights, polished metal edges, and high-frequency reflection bands.

colors.name: Sunset Amber
colors.usage: Use for warm interior key-lights, atmospheric sunset backlighting, and structural glass tinting.

colors.name: Neon Cyan
colors.usage: Use for active flight HUDs, digital dials, and engine power lines.

colors.name: Safety Orange
colors.usage: Use for piping on Vespera's flight suit, high-voltage labels, and manual release hatches.

colors.name: Deep Charcoal
colors.usage: Use for dark shadows, unlit cavities, and anti-reflective dashboard padding.

palettes.name: Raygun Gothic / Googie Retro
palettes.origin: Inspired by the bold, sweeping, space-age optimism of 1950s Googie architecture and pulp sci-fi covers.
palettes.aesthetic_notes: A retro-modern aesthetic utilizing creamy off-whites, polished brass, and atomic teals with a signature radioactive lime highlight.

colors.name: Creamy Rocket White
colors.usage: Use for external ship armor, sleek fiberglass counters, and modular ceiling panels.

colors.name: Polished Brass
colors.usage: Use for physical console dials, chrono-compass gears, and viewport trim.

colors.name: Atomic Teal
colors.usage: Use for deep control panels, cockpit seat upholstery, and secondary flight apparel.

colors.name: Radioactive Lime
colors.usage: Reserved for reactor cores, fuel conduits, and radioactive isotope gauges.

colors.name: Cherry Rocket Red
colors.usage: Use for launch buttons, warning sirens, and decorative starburst wall art.

colors.name: Bakelite Brown
colors.usage: Use for rich wood veneers, dashboard consoles, and structural base moldings.

palettes.name: Synthwave Dusk Grids
palettes.origin: Curated from late-twentieth-century electronic vector graphics and high-contrast cyberpunk sunset aesthetics.
palettes.aesthetic_notes: Extremely high contrast. Perfect for heavy neon nightlife sequences, grid-space simulations, and chrome reflection beds.

colors.name: Deep Grid Blue
colors.usage: Use for twilight skies, simulated holographic grid grounds, and shaded areas.

colors.name: Laser Magenta
colors.usage: Use for primary city light sources, neon signs, and glowing wireframe borders.

colors.name: Sunset Violet
colors.usage: Use for sky gradients, volumetric engine plumes, and holographic ambient fills.

colors.name: Electric Cyan
colors.usage: Use for secondary wireframes, radar displays, and bright reflections on visor plates.

colors.name: Solar Flare Gold
colors.usage: Use for synthetic sun shapes, glowing headlight cones, and specular surface glints.

palettes.name: Surrealist Illusion Pearl
palettes.origin: Inspired by classic surrealism (Salvador Dali, Hugo Zarate) and modern spatial-digital art.
palettes.aesthetic_notes: A shifting, ethereal palette using pearlescent base tones, liquid gold highlights, and deep amethyst shadows to disrupt physical reality.

colors.name: Iridescent Pearl
colors.usage: Use for dream-world columns, warped organic structures, and pearlescent ship hulls.

colors.name: Liquid Gold
colors.usage: Use for dripping metallic elements, glowing eyes, and celestial bodies.

colors.name: Ethereal Lilac
colors.usage: Perfect for fog, atmospheric vapor layers, and organic flesh-to-metal transitions.

colors.name: Deep Amethyst
colors.usage: Use for bottomless pits, surreal portals, and high-contrast ambient occlusion.

colors.name: Venomous Green
colors.usage: Reserved for impossible natural phenomena, temporal tears, and shifting compass needles.

workflow_integration.resolve_grading: Import these values into DaVinci Resolve's RGB Mixer. Set your primary lift/shadows to Deep Charcoal or Midnight Indigo, your midtones/gamma to Sand Drift or Slate Steel, and your gain/highlights to Polished Chrome. Clamp your key light outputs to sunset amber/gold to replicate the airbrushed luster.
workflow_integration.comfyui_prompting: When using SDXL or Flux.1, append these hex/color descriptors directly to your styling node: 'in the style of Moebius with a color scheme of sand drift #E8C595, sagebrush #78A3A2, and twilight lavender shadows #5E507A, high contrast outline, clean ink illustration'. This prevents the model's latent space from drifting into modern digital photography.

css_variables.description: Exposed CSS Variables for direct UI implementation of web storyboards.
